passage_title: Sunday Is a Fun Day for Modern Brits

sentences.sentence: In a new study, Essex University sociologists have dissected the typical British Sunday, and found we get up later and do fewer chores than we did 40 years ago - and we are far more likely to be out shopping or enjoying ourselves than cooking Sunday lunch.
sentences.translation: 在一项新研究中，埃塞克斯大学的社会学家剖析了典型的英国星期天，发现我们起得比40年前晚，做的家务也更少——而且比起做星期天的午饭，我们更可能出门购物或找乐子。

structure.text: have dissected the typical British Sunday
and found we get up later and do fewer chores than we did 40 years ago
we get up later and do fewer chores than we did 40 years ago
and we are far more likely to be out shopping or enjoying ourselves than cooking Sunday lunch

difficult_points: found 后省略了 that，引导宾语从句 we get up later...。
than we did 40 years ago 是比较状语从句，did 代替 got up 和 did chores。
be more likely to do A than B 结构中，B 部分用动名词 cooking，与前面的不定式呼应对比。

sentences.sentence: Academics at the university's Institute of Social and Economic Research asked 10,000 people to keep a detailed diary of how they spent Sundays in 2001.
sentences.translation: 该大学社会与经济研究所的学者们要求一万人详细记录他们在2001年是如何度过星期天的。

structure.text: Academics at the university's Institute of Social and Economic Research
to keep a detailed diary of how they spent Sundays in 2001

difficult_points: ask sb to do 表示要求某人做某事，to keep a diary 是不定式补足语。
how 引导的名词性从句作 diary of 的宾语，说明日记记录的内容。

sentences.sentence: Then they compared the results with 3,500 diaries written in 1961, a treasure trove of information that had been uncovered 'in two egg boxes and a tea chest' in the basement of the BBC by ISER's director, Professor Jonathan Gershuny.
sentences.translation: 接着，他们将这些结果与1961年写下的3500份日记做比较——这批珍贵的资料是由ISER主任乔纳森·格舒尼教授在BBC地下室的“两个鸡蛋盒和一个茶叶箱里”发现的。

structure.text: they compared the results with 3,500 diaries written in 1961
that had been uncovered 'in two egg boxes and a tea chest' in the basement of the BBC by ISER's director, Professor Jonathan Gershuny

difficult_points: a treasure trove of information 是 3,500 diaries 的同位语，形象地形容这些日记的价值。
that 引导定语从句修饰 information，谓语 had been uncovered 是过去完成时的被动语态。
Professor Jonathan Gershuny 是 ISER's director 的同位语。

sentences.sentence: The contrast between the two periods could not be more striking.
sentences.translation: 这两个时期之间的反差再明显不过了。

structure.text: The contrast between the two periods

difficult_points: could not be more + 形容词比较级 是双重否定的最高级表达，意为“再……不过了”。
the two periods 指代前文提到的2001年和1961年两个时期。

sentences.sentence: Forty years ago, Sunday mornings were a flurry of activity as men and women - especially women - caught up on their weekly chores and cooked up a storm in the kitchen.
sentences.translation: 四十年前，星期天早晨总是一片忙碌，男男女女——尤其是女性——忙着补做一周的家务，还在厨房里大展厨艺。

structure.text: Sunday mornings were a flurry of activity
caught up on their weekly chores and cooked up a storm in the kitchen

difficult_points: as 引导方式/时间状语从句，说明星期天早晨忙碌的具体情形。
especially women 是插入语，强调女性尤其如此。
catch up on 与 cook up a storm 是并列谓语，前者指补做家务，后者指大展厨艺。

sentences.sentence: Women rarely allowed themselves any'leisure' until the afternoon, after the dishes were cleaned.
sentences.translation: 女性很少在下午之前——也就是碗碟都洗完之后——给自己片刻“休闲”时间。
difficult_points: rarely...until 表示“直到……才”，强调女性很少在下午之前给自己休闲时间。
any'leisure' 中缺少的空格是原文排版问题，实为 any 'leisure'。
after the dishes were cleaned 是被动语态的时间状语从句，补充说明“下午”的具体条件。

sentences.sentence: In 1961, more than a fifth of all men and women in Britain were sitting at a table by 2 p.m., most likely tucking into a roast with all the trimmings.
sentences.translation: 1961年，超过五分之一的英国男女到下午两点前就已经坐在餐桌旁，多半正在大快朵颐地享用配菜齐全的烤肉大餐。

structure.text: more than a fifth of all men and women in Britain
were sitting at a table by 2 p.m.
most likely tucking into a roast with all the trimmings

difficult_points: more than a fifth of 表示“超过五分之一”。
by 2 p.m. 表示“到下午两点之前”，强调截止时间。
tucking into... 现在分词短语补充说明这些人当时的动作，逻辑主语是前面的主语。

sentences.sentence: Then there would be another rush to the table between 5 p.m. and 6 p.m. for high tea.
sentences.translation: 接着，五点到六点之间又会有一次涌向餐桌的高峰，为的是吃晚茶。

structure.text: there would be another rush to the table

difficult_points: would be 表示过去的习惯性动作，意为“那时候总会有……”。
for high tea 说明这次涌向餐桌的目的是吃下午茶（英式晚茶）。

expressions.meaning_zh: 英式晚茶（傍晚较丰盛的一餐）

sentences.sentence: Since the arrival of brunch, the gastropub and the all-you-can-eat Sunday buffet at the local curry house, such institutions have become extinct.
sentences.translation: 自从早午餐、美食酒吧和当地咖喱馆的星期天自助餐兴起以来，这些传统的用餐习惯已经消失殆尽。

structure.text: Since the arrival of brunch, the gastropub and the all-you-can-eat Sunday buffet at the local curry house

difficult_points: Since 引导时间状语，列举 brunch、gastropub、自助餐三种新兴餐饮形式的出现。
such institutions 指代前文提到的传统星期天用餐习惯。

sentences.sentence: Today, we graze the entire day.
sentences.translation: 如今，我们一整天都在零零散散地吃东西。
difficult_points: graze 本义指牲畜“吃草”，此处比喻人们一整天不停零食进食的状态。
the entire day 作时间状语，修饰 graze 持续的时长。

sentences.sentence: You only have two free days a week.
sentences.translation: 每周你只有两天休息日。

structure.text: only have two free days a week

difficult_points: You 是泛指用法，指代任何人，相当于“人们”。
two free days a week 说明每周休息日的数量。

sentences.sentence: You don't want to have to waste one because there is nothing to do but watch TV.
sentences.translation: 你不想白白浪费掉一天，因为除了看电视就无事可做。

structure.text: You don't want to have to waste one
because there is nothing to do but watch TV

difficult_points: one 指代前句提到的 free day。
nothing to do but do sth 表示“除了……无事可做”，but 在此作介词，后接省略 to 的动词原形 watch。

sentences.sentence: Sunday has leapfrogged Saturday in the fun stakes.
sentences.translation: 在“有趣程度”的较量中，星期天已经反超了星期六。
difficult_points: leapfrog 本义为“跳背游戏”，此处用作动词，比喻“反超、跃居……之上”。
in the ... stakes 是习语，指“在……的较量中”。

sentences.sentence: On Saturdays, you are recovering from the week.
sentences.translation: 星期六，你还在从这一周的疲惫中恢复过来。

structure.text: you are recovering from the week

difficult_points: are recovering 是现在进行时，强调星期六仍处于从一周疲惫中恢复的状态。
from the week 说明恢复的对象是整周的疲惫。

sentences.sentence: Sundays are the last bastion of the weekend - you want to get as much as you can out of the day before you have to go back to work.
sentences.translation: 星期天是周末最后的堡垒——在不得不回去上班之前，你想要尽可能地好好享受这一天。

structure.text: Sundays are the last bastion of the weekend
you want to get as much as you can out of the day
before you have to go back to work

difficult_points: the last bastion of 是比喻用法，意为“最后的堡垒”，强调星期天是周末仅剩的珍贵时光。
as much as you can 插入在 get...out of the day 中间，表示尽可能多地享受这一天。
before 引导时间状语从句，说明这种“抓紧享受”的期限。

expressions.meaning_zh: 充分利用；从……中获得最大收益

sentences.sentence: According to researchers, the ability to trail around B&Q has made the most dramatic difference to our Sundays.
sentences.translation: 研究人员表示，能够在B&Q之类的家居卖场里闲逛，是让我们的星期天发生最大变化的原因。

structure.text: the ability to trail around B&Q
has made the most dramatic difference to our Sundays

difficult_points: to trail around B&Q 是不定式短语，修饰 ability，B&Q 是英国连锁家居建材超市名称。
make a difference to 表示对……产生影响，the most dramatic 强调影响之大。

sentences.sentence: In 1961, adults spent an average of 20 minutes a day shopping; by 2001, it was 50 minutes.
sentences.translation: 1961年，成年人平均每天花20分钟购物；到2001年，这一数字变成了50分钟。

structure.text: adults spent an average of 20 minutes a day shopping

difficult_points: spend + 时间 + doing 表示花费时间做某事。
分号连接两个对比句，it 指代前面提到的每天购物时间。

sentences.sentence: 'Shopping used to be a gender segregated activity that would take place during the week, while the husband was at work.
sentences.translation: “购物过去是一件按性别划分的活动，通常在工作日、丈夫上班的时候进行。

structure.text: used to be a gender segregated activity
that would take place during the week
while the husband was at work

difficult_points: used to be 表示过去经常性的状态，暗示现在已不同。
that 引导定语从句修饰 activity，would 表示过去的习惯性动作。
while 引导时间状语从句，说明购物通常发生在丈夫上班的时候。

sentences.sentence: Now it's as much men as women,' said Gershuny.
sentences.translation: 如今男性和女性购物的一样多了，”格舒尼说道。

structure.text: Now it's as much men as women

difficult_points: as much A as B 表示“A和B一样多”，此处指男性和女性购物人数相当。
it 指代前一句提到的购物这件事。

sentences.sentence: We're all more likely to be relaxing or shopping on a Sunday morning these days than scrubbing the floor or putting up shelves.
sentences.translation: 如今我们星期天早上更可能是在放松或购物，而不是在擦地板或安装置物架。

structure.text: We're all more likely to be relaxing or shopping on a Sunday morning these days
than scrubbing the floor or putting up shelves

difficult_points: be more likely to do A than B 结构中，B 部分用动名词 scrubbing/putting 与前面呼应。
putting up shelves 指安装置物架，是家务劳动的例子。

sentences.sentence: 'Men now stay in bed longer, and get up not, as previously, to work around the house, but rather to shop or to pursue other outside leisure activities.'
sentences.translation: “现在男性赖床更久了，起床也不再像过去那样是为了在家里做家务，而是为了去购物或从事其他户外休闲活动。”

structure.text: Men now stay in bed longer
and get up not, as previously, to work around the house, but rather to shop or to pursue other outside leisure activities
but rather to shop or to pursue other outside leisure activities
to pursue other outside leisure activities

difficult_points: not A but rather B 表示“不是A，而是B”，说明起床目的的转变。
as previously 是插入语，等于 as they did previously，与现在做对比。
to work around the house 与 to shop/to pursue... 是并列的不定式，分别代表过去和现在起床的目的。

sentences.sentence: Men do about the same amount of unpaid work around the house as they used to on a Sunday, but it's spread throughout the whole day, instead of crammed into the morning.
sentences.translation: 男性星期天做的无偿家务活总量和过去差不多，只是现在分散在了一整天，而不是集中在早上。

structure.text: Men do about the same amount of unpaid work around the house
as they used to on a Sunday
but it's spread throughout the whole day
instead of crammed into the morning

difficult_points: as they used to 省略了 do unpaid work around the house，与主句作比较。
it 指代前文的 unpaid work，is spread 是被动语态。
instead of crammed... 省略了 being，与 spread throughout the whole day 形成对比。

sentences.sentence: Women do considerably less than 40 years ago.
sentences.translation: 而女性做的家务比40年前要少得多。
difficult_points: do considerably less 省略了 unpaid work around the house，承接上句。
than 40 years ago 省略了 they did，构成比较状语。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, men and women were 'pretty much different species' in 1961, as far as the way they spent Sundays was concerned, with men far more likely to be out of the house - at the pub or playing football - before lunch.
sentences.translation: 确实，就星期天的度过方式而言，1961年的男性和女性“几乎是两种不同的物种”，男性午餐前更可能不在家里——待在酒吧或踢足球。

structure.text: men and women were 'pretty much different species' in 1961
as far as the way they spent Sundays was concerned
with men far more likely to be out of the house
at the pub or playing football

difficult_points: as far as ... is concerned 表示“就……而言”。
with men far more likely to be out of the house 是 with 独立主格结构，省略 being。
破折号中的 at the pub or playing football 举例说明男性外出的具体活动。

sentences.sentence: 'For women, leisure happened only in the afternoon.
sentences.translation: “对女性来说，休闲只发生在下午。

structure.text: leisure happened only in the afternoon

difficult_points: For women 是话题状语，引出下文对女性休闲时间的说明。
only in the afternoon 强调女性休闲仅限于下午。

sentences.sentence: But by 2001, the shapes of men's and women's Sundays were much more similar,' says the report.
sentences.translation: 但到2001年，男性和女性星期天的度过方式已经变得相似得多了，”报告中写道。

structure.text: the shapes of men's and women's Sundays were much more similar

difficult_points: the shapes of ... Sundays 比喻男女度过星期天的“形态/模式”。
much more similar 中 much 修饰比较级，加强相似程度。

sentences.sentence: 'Sunday for me is all about holding on to the weekend and trying to stave off Monday.
sentences.translation: “对我来说，星期天的意义就在于抓住周末的尾巴，尽量推迟星期一的到来。

structure.text: is all about holding on to the weekend and trying to stave off Monday
and trying to stave off Monday

difficult_points: be all about doing sth 表示“核心/关键在于……”。
hold on to 与 stave off 是并列的动名词短语，分别表示“抓住不放”和“抵挡、推迟”。

sentences.sentence: An ideal Sunday would involve getting up and having a nice lunch.
sentences.translation: 理想的星期天应该包括睡到自然醒，然后美美地吃一顿午饭。

structure.text: would involve getting up and having a nice lunch
getting up and having a nice lunch

difficult_points: would 表示对理想星期天的假设性描述。
involve doing sth 后接并列动名词，说明理想星期天包含的内容。

sentences.sentence: Sometimes we cook, but more often I go out to get a roast or bangers and mash at a gastropub.
sentences.translation: 有时候我们自己做饭，但更多时候我会出门去美食酒吧点一份烤肉或者香肠土豆泥。

structure.text: but more often I go out
to get a roast or bangers and mash at a gastropub

difficult_points: sometimes...but more often 对比两种频率不同的做法。
bangers and mash 是英式俚语，指“香肠土豆泥”。

expressions.meaning_zh: 香肠配土豆泥（英式家常菜）

sentences.sentence: If it is a nice day, there is nothing better than sitting outside in the beer garden, reading the Sunday papers - one tabloid and one broadsheet - with a Guinness, extra cold.
sentences.translation: 如果天气好，没有什么比坐在啤酒花园里，读着星期天的报纸——一份小报一份大报——再配一杯冰镇健力士啤酒更惬意的了。

structure.text: If it is a nice day
there is nothing better than sitting outside in the beer garden

difficult_points: nothing better than doing sth 表示“没有什么比……更好”。
reading the Sunday papers 与 sitting outside 并列，说明具体活动。
one tabloid and one broadsheet 是同位语，说明两份报纸的类型（小报和大报）。

sentences.sentence: Sunday is often a chance to visit other parts of London, as long as it is not too far.
sentences.translation: 星期天常常是去伦敦其他地方逛逛的好机会，只要不是太远。

structure.text: is often a chance to visit other parts of London
to visit other parts of London
as long as it is not too far

difficult_points: a chance to do 中不定式修饰 chance，说明机会的具体内容。
as long as 引导条件状语从句，it 指代要去的地方。

sentences.sentence: I use Sundays to go clothes shopping, or to the cinema.
sentences.translation: 我会利用星期天去买衣服，或者去看电影。

structure.text: to go clothes shopping, or to the cinema

difficult_points: use sth to do 表示利用某段时间做某事。
or to the cinema 承前省略了 to go，完整应为 or to go to the cinema。

sentences.sentence: I often go to Camden market, as I love the international foods on offer and hunting for bargains and vintage clothes.'
sentences.translation: 我经常去卡姆登市场，因为我喜欢那里供应的各国美食，也喜欢淘便宜货和复古服装。

structure.text: I often go to Camden market
as I love the international foods on offer and hunting for bargains and vintage clothes
hunting for bargains and vintage clothes

difficult_points: as 引导原因状语从句，love 后接并列宾语（名词短语和动名词短语）。
on offer 后置修饰 foods，表示“供应的、在售的”。
hunting for bargains and vintage clothes 与 the international foods 并列作 love 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: Jonathan Bentley Atchison (25, Clapham, London, works in communications)
sentences.translation: 乔纳森·本特利·阿奇森（25岁，伦敦克拉珀姆，从事传媒工作）
difficult_points: 括号内以逗号分隔列出年龄、居住地和职业，是人物简介的固定写法。
works in communications 说明其从事传媒/公关相关工作。

expressions.meaning_zh: 从事传媒/公关行业工作

sentences.sentence: 'I am usually at home making the Sunday lunch.
sentences.translation: “我通常在家做星期天的午饭。
difficult_points: making the Sunday lunch 是现在分词短语，补充说明在家时正在做的事情。
usually 修饰 am at home，说明这是常态而非偶尔情况。

sentences.sentence: Some friends go out to eat, but my husband Mark loves a roast, so we don't.
sentences.translation: 有些朋友会出去吃饭，但我丈夫马克喜欢吃烤肉，所以我们不出去。

structure.text: Some friends go out to eat
but my husband Mark loves a roast

difficult_points: so we don't 省略了 go out to eat，承接第一分句。
my husband Mark 是同位语结构，说明丈夫姓名。

sentences.sentence: After that, I do the washing, like every day, and then I take my daughter Grace to netball and watch her play.
sentences.translation: 那之后，我会像平常一样洗衣服，然后带女儿格蕾丝去打无挡板篮球，看她比赛。

structure.text: and then I take my daughter Grace to netball and watch her play

difficult_points: like every day 是插入的比较状语，说明洗衣服是每天都做的事。
take sb to... and watch her play 是并列谓语，均以 I 为主语。

sentences.sentence: Mark potters around - last Sunday, he tidied the garage.
sentences.translation: 马克则在家里悠闲地做些零碎事——上星期天他收拾了车库。

structure.text: last Sunday, he tidied the garage

difficult_points: potter around 表示悠闲地做些零碎事，没有明确目标。
破折号后举例说明 potters around 的具体内容。

sentences.sentence: He works six days a week, so on Sunday he stays at home.
sentences.translation: 他一周工作六天，所以星期天就待在家里。

structure.text: He works six days a week
so on Sunday he stays at home

difficult_points: so 引导结果状语从句，说明因为平时工作繁忙所以星期天留在家。
six days a week 说明每周工作天数。

sentences.sentence: I don't like shopping on a Sunday because every man and his dog is out.
sentences.translation: 我不喜欢星期天购物，因为那时候几乎所有人都出门了。

structure.text: I don't like shopping on a Sunday
because every man and his dog is out

difficult_points: every man and his dog 是习语，夸张地表示“几乎所有人”。
because 引导原因状语从句，解释不喜欢星期天购物的理由。

sentences.sentence: I don't work, so I can do it in the week.
sentences.translation: 我不上班，所以可以在工作日去购物。

structure.text: so I can do it in the week

difficult_points: it 指代前文的 shopping。
in the week 指工作日期间（与周末相对）。

sentences.sentence: I tend to watch television and chill out.
sentences.translation: 我通常会看看电视，放松一下。

structure.text: tend to watch television and chill out

difficult_points: tend to do 表示“倾向于、通常会做”。
chill out 是口语表达，意为放松、休息。

sentences.sentence: When summer comes, we go to barbecues at family or friends'houses.
sentences.translation: 夏天到了，我们就去家人或朋友家参加烧烤聚会。

structure.text: we go to barbecues at family or friends'houses

difficult_points: friends'houses 中缺少空格，应为 friends' houses，属于原文排版问题。
go to barbecues at 表示去参加在某处举办的烧烤聚会。

sentences.sentence: When I was growing up, my dad would do the gardening and paint the fences while my mum would do housework.'
sentences.translation: “我小时候，我爸爸会打理花园、粉刷篱笆，而我妈妈则会做家务。”

structure.text: my dad would do the gardening and paint the fences
while my mum would do housework

difficult_points: would 在这里表示过去反复发生的习惯性动作。
while 引导对比状语从句，将父亲和母亲当时分工不同的家务进行对照。

sentences.sentence: Hazel Hallows (42, Manchester, housewife, married with three children)
sentences.translation: 黑兹尔·哈洛斯（42岁，曼彻斯特，家庭主妇，已婚并育有三个孩子）

structure.text: (42, Manchester, housewife, married with three children)

difficult_points: married with three children 说明其已婚并育有三个孩子，是人物简介的固定表达。
housewife 说明其职业身份为家庭主妇。

sentences.sentence: 'When we were at home, I would get out in the garden, and my late wife Rose would cook the Sunday lunch and do the housework.
sentences.translation: “我们在家的时候，我会到花园里忙活，我已故的妻子罗丝会做星期天的午饭，还会做家务。

structure.text: I would get out in the garden
and my late wife Rose would cook the Sunday lunch and do the housework

difficult_points: would 表示过去反复的习惯性动作。
my late wife Rose 中 late 表示“已故的”，说明妻子已经去世。

sentences.sentence: I was an engineer, and Rose worked full-time as a supermarket manageress.
sentences.translation: 我曾是工程师，罗丝则全职担任超市女经理。

structure.text: and Rose worked full-time as a supermarket manageress

difficult_points: work as 表示“担任……职位”，full-time 修饰工作性质为全职。
manageress 是 manager 的阴性形式，指女经理。

sentences.sentence: In 1961, we had just moved to Bristol, and I spent Sunday maintaining the new house.
sentences.translation: 1961年，我们刚搬到布里斯托尔，我把星期天都花在了打理新房子上。

structure.text: we had just moved to Bristol
and I spent Sunday maintaining the new house

difficult_points: had just moved 是过去完成时，表示搬家发生在那段星期天生活之前不久。
spend + 时间 + doing 表示花费时间做某事。

sentences.sentence: The washing and ironing had to be done - it was a working-together atmosphere.
sentences.translation: 洗衣熨衣这些活儿总得有人做——那是一种齐心协力的氛围。
difficult_points: had to be done 是被动语态，强调洗衣熨烫这些活是必须完成的任务。
a working-together atmosphere 是复合形容词修饰的名词短语，指“共同劳作的氛围”。

sentences.sentence: We would sometimes go and spend the day with Rose's sister or other relatives.
sentences.translation: 我们有时候会去和罗丝的姐妹或其他亲戚一起过一天。

structure.text: would sometimes go and spend the day with Rose's sister or other relatives

difficult_points: would sometimes 表示过去偶尔发生的习惯性动作。
go and spend the day with 是并列动词结构，表示一同前往并度过一天。

sentences.sentence: In 1961, it was the first time I had a new car, so we spent time in the countryside or garden centres.
sentences.translation: 1961年，那是我第一次拥有一辆新车，所以我们常常开车去乡下或者园艺中心度过时光。

structure.text: it was the first time I had a new car
so we spent time in the countryside or garden centres

difficult_points: it was the first time (that) 后省略了 that，从句修饰 the first time，说明具体是哪一次。
so 引导结果状语从句，说明有新车之后的活动变化。

sentences.sentence: Now, I get up on Sundays and spend a couple of hours reading the newspapers.'
sentences.translation: “现在，我星期天起床后，会花几个小时看报纸。”

structure.text: I get up on Sundays and spend a couple of hours reading the newspapers

difficult_points: get up 与 spend 是并列谓语，共同以 I 为主语。
spend + 时间 + doing 表示花时间做某事。

sentences.sentence: Bryan Jones (79, pensioner, Frampton Cotterell, near Bristol)
sentences.translation: 布莱恩·琼斯（79岁，退休人员，居住在布里斯托尔附近的弗兰普顿·科特雷尔）
difficult_points: pensioner 表示“领养老金者、退休人员”。
near Bristol 修饰 Frampton Cotterell，说明该地靠近布里斯托尔。
